source: pandapower / MATPOWER PEGASE cases (BSD); raw grids not redistributed
meanfield_control_note: The degree-matched configuration-model control is unreliable for these grids because of their heavy degree-1/degree-2 tail (17-39% leaves), so the theoretical mean-field value 2.5 is used as the reference rather than the per-grid control.
interpretation: ROBUST: the CEBH transfer failure generalizes to a second, non-road embedded infrastructure. On three PEGASE European transmission grids the degree-moment formula under-predicts the observed threshold by ~0.37 on average (larger than the ~0.22 road gap), and the junction-composition anchor cuts the absolute error ~4-fold even though power grids are non-planar. SUGGESTIVE: the cluster-size-distribution exponent is intermediate (~2.2-2.4) between near-planar roads (~2.12, close to the 2D value 2.055) and the mean-field value 2.5, consistent with embedding planarity grading the universality signature; the single-grid estimates are noisier than the road population, so this is read as supporting context. This is a three-grid minimal replication, not a population study.
